title: ophrys fusca subsp. creberrima
intro: Ophrys fusca subsp. creberrima, also known as the Bee Orchid, is a subspecies of the Early Spider-orchid, a type of flowering plant that belongs to the orchid family (Orchidaceae). This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Ophrys fusca subsp. creberrima include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties and health benefits.
